things.name: Borisov Town Centre
things.description: Borisov's historic centre on the Berezina River with the Resurrection Cathedral and tree-lined promenade; a compact Belarusian town rebuilt after WWII devastation

things.name: Battle of the Berezina Memorial
things.description: Napoleon's catastrophic 1812 river crossing where the Grande Armée suffered its most devastating rearguard action; monuments mark this pivotal moment in European history

things.name: Resurrection Cathedral
things.description: Borisov's principal Orthodox church with gilded domes visible across the city; rebuilt in post-Soviet years on the foundations of the historic 19th-century cathedral

things.name: Berezina River Park
things.description: Scenic riverside park along the Berezina with walking paths, summer beach and water sports; the river gives Borisov much of its character and historical significance

things.name: Local History Museum
things.description: Borisov regional museum covering the Battle of the Berezina, WWII occupation and local Belarusian history and culture through extensive exhibits

things.name: Cathedral of the Nativity of Christ
things.description: Roman Catholic church in Borisov reflecting the town's mixed historical heritage; an attractive neo-Gothic building serving the local Catholic community

things.name: Victory Park
things.description: Soviet-era memorial park with WWII monuments and the Eternal Flame; the main civic green space of Borisov used for national celebrations and leisure

things.name: Minsk (day trip)
things.description: Belarus's capital is just 75km from Borisov; Stalin's monumental showpiece city features the sweeping Independence Avenue, National Library and vibrant old town

things.name: Borisov Market
things.description: Central market offering traditional Belarusian produce, crafts and street food; a lively snapshot of everyday Belarusian life and a great place to sample local cuisine

things.name: Borisov Arena
things.description: Modern 13,126-seat stadium home of BATE Borisov, Belarus's most decorated club with 16 consecutive national titles and multiple UEFA Champions League group stage appearances
